Ptosis due to levator palpebrae superioris weakness.
Pupillary involvement is spared because the parasympathetic fibers are superficial and less affected by ischemia.

The question: "most likely diagnosis" - likely "diabetic third nerve palsy" or "ischemic oculomotor nerve palsy due to diabetes."
So answer: "Diabetic third cranial nerve palsy (ischemic oculomotor nerve palsy)".
